definition: Computational derivation verification refutation or exploration of formal claims.
Application of explicit formal rules to premises without hidden judgment during execution.
A stated assumption fact constraint or axiom used in inference.
A valid step for deriving conclusions from premises.
Searching for or checking formal proofs.
Determining whether a Boolean formula has a satisfying assignment.
Satisfiability solving modulo background theories such as arithmetic or arrays.
Exploring formal states to verify properties or find counterexamples.
Representing computation through facts rules and queries.
A system for checking machine-readable proofs
